---
title: "Disable UXA?"
date: 2009-04-27
forum: General Help
---

### Post by Stevo_92 on 2009-04-27
I decided to try out UXA with my Intel 4500MHD but i noticed very little improvement and it makes my system crash every once and a while. So i was wondering, how would i go back to exa? I don't want to mess around with it without knowing what I'm doing because I've only been using using Ubuntu for about 2 weeks now and don't want to mess anything up.

here's my xorg.conf file:
```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# Note that some configuration settings that could be done previously
# in this file, now are automatically configured by the server and settings
# here are ignored.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Configured Video Device"
    Option       "AccelMethod"   "uxa"  
       Option       "EXAOptimizeMigration" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Default Screen"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    Device        "Configured Video Device"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by spiderbatdad on 2009-04-27
cd into /etc/X11 and run ls -al
look for a backup of xorg.conf
copy it to the current.

---

### Post by Stevo_92 on 2009-04-27
awesome, thank you

---

### Post by Arup on 2009-04-27
[https://wiki.ubuntu.com/X/UxaTesting](https://wiki.ubuntu.com/X/UxaTesting)

According to this wiki this is all you add

Section "Device"
        Identifier    "Configured Video Device"
        # ...
        Option        "AccelMethod" "uxa"
EndSection

but in your case I see an extra line mentioning "EXAOptimizeMigration" "true" and maybe that could be the reason for your crashes.

Currently on my G31 chipset I use the latest stable xorg from [https://edge.launchpad.net/~ubuntu-x-swat/+archive/x-updates/](https://edge.launchpad.net/~ubuntu-x-swat/+archive/x-updates/) and have added the video RAM number to my xorg, my PC has been very stable, no crashes, lock ups, Google Earth runs well, no high CPU usage when watching movies full screen.

---
